ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pizza class (FOODON_00002572); no specific sourdough-base triple-cheese jalapeño pizza subclass exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a broad category. No specific code for sourdough-base triple-cheese variant; closest parent match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept found for this pizza variant; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one slice of approximately 100 g, providing around 307 kcal, 16 g protein, 23 g fat, and 9 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Dynamite Cheese Pizza — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database record for Dynamite Cheese (cr_id G00010630), a canonical Singapore food record with confidence 0.95 and review status 'reviewed'. All per-100g values are preserved as provided.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Pizza, cheese, sourdough base
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Dynamite Cheese Pizza specifically. GI of 57 (medium) is estimated from the closest analogous food: cheese pizza on a sourdough-style base, as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and consistent with Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Sourdough fermentation lowers the GI of the bread base relative to standard refined-flour pizza bases (typical GI 60–80). GL per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 57 × 8.85 / 100 ≈ 5.0 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium at 1279 mg/100 g — more than double the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this food; even a 100 g portion delivers 1279 mg sodium, representing over 55% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg, and the combination of processed mozzarella, cheddar, and parmesan makes sodium reduction by substitution impractical.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (8.85 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre and high saturated fat (~12.5 g/100 g) impairing insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit this food to small portions; the absence of dietary fibre removes any buffering of the glycaemic response from the sourdough base, and the high saturated fat content from three processed cheeses contributes to chronic insulin resistance when consumed regularly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~12.5 g/100 g), very high sodium (1279 mg/100 g), and elevated cholesterol (78 mg/100 g) present a combined cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption; the triple-cheese combination delivers approximately 12.5 g saturated fat per 100 g — exceeding the 5 g/100 g threshold — alongside 1279 mg sodium and 78 mg cholesterol, all of which independently and synergistically elevate cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (~12.5 g/100 g) exceeding the 5 g/100 g RA threshold, compounded by high sodium promoting tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food; the saturated fat from processed cheese exceeds the RA threshold and promotes systemic inflammation, while the 1279 mg/100 g sodium load exacerbates joint swelling and fluid retention.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dynamite Cheese Pizza is a baked pizza dish available in Singapore, featuring a sourdough-fermented base topped with Italian tomato pizza sauce and a generous triple-cheese blend of mozzarella, cheddar, and parmesan, finished with sliced jalapeños for heat. The sourdough base contributes a mildly tangy, chewy texture, while the combination of three cheeses delivers a rich, salty, and intensely savoury flavour profile. The jalapeños add a moderate spicy kick that contrasts with the creamy, melted cheese. This dish is classified as vegetarian. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at 307 kcal per 100 g, with a high fat content of 23 g (predominantly saturated fat estimated at ~12.5 g from the cheese blend) and a moderate protein content of 16 g. Carbohydrate is relatively low at 8.85 g per 100 g, but dietary fibre is absent (0 g), meaning there is no buffering of the glycaemic response. Sodium is extremely high at 1279 mg per 100 g, driven by the processed cheese and sauce components, making this food strongly contraindicated for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Calcium is notably high at 1375 mg per 100 g, reflecting the dense cheese content. The glycaemic index is estimated at 57 (medium) based on the sourdough base, which fermentation lowers relative to standard refined-flour pizza bases. This dish is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner in Singapore and is associated with casual dining and pizza restaurant menus.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010630), a canonical Singapore food record with confidence 0.95 and review status 'reviewed'. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. Saturated fat is estimated at ~12.5 g/100 g based on the known composition of the triple-cheese blend (mozzarella, cheddar, parmesan) as cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central cheese entries, which is the only field not directly reported by HPB.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero (0.0) per HPB source record; this is a processed pizza product with a refined sourdough base and no fibre-contributing vegetables beyond jalapeños in trace amounts. 2. saturated_fat_g of 12.5 g/100 g is estimated from the triple-cheese composition (mozzarella ~5 g, cheddar ~4 g, parmesan ~3.5 g per 100 g of combined product) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for each cheese type; this field is not directly reported in the HPB source. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and micronutrient panel beyond those reported are not available in the HPB Foodo database record for this specific dish as of 2026-04-16. 4. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on sourdough cheese pizza from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in approved sources. 5. Halal status is not confirmed; the HPB record does not carry a halal certification flag for this item.
